sources.label: Ingevity (NGVT) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript - The Motley Fool (Jun 1, pre-window)
Ingevity IR - News Releases (no releases in Jun 6-9 window)
Ingevity 8-K - Ozark road markings sale to PPG closed Apr 15, 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already pushed it down to reconcile the adjusted-score gap (rank ~49-50 vs old displayed 32); nothing new since June 6 and a +1.99% drift day does not change the strict-screen setup, so hold at the corrected rank with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Post-divestiture Ingevity is a cleaner, deleveraging activated-carbon pure-play with $160M of cash proceeds and $300M buybacks by 2027; any concrete data-center filtration/adsorption or battery-adjacent design win would rerate a $2.3B under-followed name.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 FY2026 (sales $258M, net income $59.8M, guidance reaffirmed) landed June 1 and is already in the price; the AI pathway remains speculative optionality with no disclosed AI-infrastructure revenue, consistent with a deep rank-49 latent slot.
verdicts.bear: The portfolio reshaping concentrates the company on automotive emissions canisters and pavement technologies - arguably moving it further from any AI-compute bottleneck role - so the latent-node thesis may never activate while auto-cycle exposure dominates.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named filtration/adsorption or battery-materials win tied to data-center or energy-storage infrastructure, or Q2 earnings showing growth in non-auto activated carbon applications.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Thesis stays latent: auto-driven activated carbon demand defines the stock and the strict AI screen eventually drops a name with no demonstrable AI linkage.
